text.title: Turborepo Anti-patterns and Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Circular dependencies, missing outputs, v1/v2 config confusion, remote cache misconfig, and AI-agent-specific failure modes.

antipattern.description: Circular workspace dependencies: packages/a depends on packages/b which depends on packages/a.
antipattern.reason: Turborepo cannot build a task graph with a cycle; the build hangs or fails with a cryptic error. Extract shared code into a third packages/shared with no deps.

antipattern.description: Missing dependsOn on typecheck or lint tasks.
antipattern.reason: Without dependsOn: ["^build"], Turborepo may run typecheck before the upstream build emits declaration files, producing false type errors.

antipattern.description: Adding dev server to the cache with outputs: [].
antipattern.reason: Dev servers are non-deterministic; caching them causes Turborepo to serve a stale server on next run. Set cache: false, persistent: true.

rule.statement: Always use the tasks field in new turbo.json; treat any pipeline field as a migration signal to update.
rule.rationale: Most training data for agents predates Turborepo 2.x. Agents will generate pipeline-based config that silently produces wrong behavior on v2.

example.note: v1 syntax; silently accepted but may misbehave on turbo v2.

section.title: Remote Cache Gotchas

rule.statement: Store TURBO_TOKEN in GitHub Secrets, never in vars; set signature: true in turbo.json remoteCache only when secretKey is also configured.
rule.rationale: Token in vars is readable by any workflow run; signature: true without a secretKey is a no-op that provides false security assurance.

section.reference: GitHub Actions cache step for node_modules, .turbo, and .next/cache.

antipattern.description: Agent edits pnpm-lock.yaml by hand to resolve a merge conflict.
antipattern.reason: Manual edits corrupt integrity hashes; always re-run pnpm install to regenerate the lockfile.

antipattern.description: Agent uses --filter=@monorepo/web... (three dots) thinking it selects the package and its dependencies.
antipattern.reason: Three trailing dots means "package and its dependents" (reverse deps), not dependencies. Use ^... for dependencies, ... for dependents.
